cot_prefix: Okay, let's think through this step by step. So the question is whether following these instructions will bring you back to the starting point. The person is always facing forward, but the directions given are right, backward, left, forward, right again. Hmm, right and left are relative to the direction you're facing, right? So if you're always facing forward, then right and left would be to your right and left sides regardless of any turns. Wait, but the problem says "always face forward," which I think means that you never turn; you keep the same orientation throughout. So your forward direction remains the same the whole time. Let me visualize this. Let's assume you start at origin (0,0) on a coordinate plane. Let's say forward is along the positive y-axis. So right would be the positive x-direction, left would be the negative x-direction, and forward is the positive y-direction, backward is the negative y-direction. So the steps
